---
name: Language Support Request
about: Request support for a new language in CircleOne
labels: language-support, enhancement
---

## Language Name

**Language name**: (e.g., Chichewa, Shona, Swahili)
**Endonym**: (the name in the language itself, e.g., chiCheŵa)
**ISO 639 code**: (e.g., ny, sn, sw)

## Language Family

**Family**: (e.g., Bantu / Niger-Congo)
**Branch**: (e.g., Zone N, Zone S, Zone G)
**Approximate number of speakers**:

## Relationship to Existing Languages

Which already-supported languages is this language most closely related to? This helps us estimate how many new glyphs will be needed.

**Closest supported language**: (e.g., "closest to isiZulu" or "closest to Sesotho")

## Unique Phonemes

List phonemes in this language that are NOT already in the CircleOne syllable inventory. Refer to `docs/syllable-inventory.md` for the current inventory.

| Phoneme (Latin) | IPA | Category | Example word | Translation |
|-----------------|-----|----------|--------------|-------------|
|                 |     |          |              |             |
|                 |     |          |              |             |

If you are unsure which phonemes are missing, describe the language's consonant and vowel systems and we will compare.

## Vowel System

How many vowels does this language distinguish?

- [ ] 5 vowels (a, e, i, o, u) -- same as isiZulu
- [ ] 7 vowels (adds open-mid E, O) -- like Tshivenda
- [ ] Other (describe below)

**Vowel length**: Does the language distinguish long and short vowels?
**Tone**: Is the language tonal? If so, how many tones?

## Click Consonants

Does this language use click consonants?

- [ ] Yes -- same inventory as isiZulu/isiXhosa
- [ ] Yes -- different click inventory (describe below)
- [ ] No

## Reference Materials

Provide at least one reference that documents the language's phonology:

- **Grammar or phonology description**: (title, author, year)
- **Dictionary**: (title, publisher)
- **Online resource**: (URL)
- **Native speaker contact**: (optional, if you are a native speaker or can connect us with one)

## Writing System Status

Is this language currently written in Latin script? Are there any existing alternative scripts or orthography debates?

## Additional Notes

Any other information that would help us evaluate and plan support for this language.
